summary: Phase 6 testing complete. Backend 17/17 new tests + 53/53 regression tests all PASS (70/70 total). All Phase 6 endpoints validated end-to-end: GET /api/reports/eod-cash-drawer (PDF + RBAC), POST /api/clients with extended EHR fields + auto-MRN (NMS-{6 hex}) + GET round-trip, /api/analytics/overview notes_by_provider shape (N+1 -> $in fix verified), /api/visits/{id}/chat (RBAC 403 + 404), /api/visits/{id}/recording (multipart upload, RBAC, GridFS persistence), and /api/ws/visit/{id} WebSocket signaling (close codes 4401/4404/4403 + chat persistence to db.visit_chat). Frontend: AppointmentsEHR week view with Up Next strip + 84 hour-cells, NewAppointmentDialog, side drawer with 4 workflow buttons, AddPatientWizard (4-step stepper + create + MRN column), Transactions EOD button — all working. Two real bugs were found and fixed (see updated_files).
action_items: Verify NewAppointmentDialog Mode select payload value: SelectItem options use 'in_clinic' / 'telehealth' but backend AppointmentIn.visit_mode Literal expects 'in_person' / 'telehealth' — submitting 'in_clinic' will 422. Suggest changing the SelectItem value to 'in_person' (display label can stay 'In clinic').
Cosmetic: TelehealthVisit header still reads appt.start_time instead of appt.start (carried over from iteration_2; one-line fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/TelehealthVisit.jsx).

Optional hardening: WS handler currently lets a 2nd practitioner take over by closing the first with code 4000 — works as designed, but consider returning a 'session-replaced' message to the displaced peer for UX clarity.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIXED by tester (was blocking all WS connections): /app/backend/server.py ws_visit() called decode_token(token, kind='access') but auth_utils.decode_token signature is (token: str). All valid tokens were rejected, the server returned HTTP 403 at handshake. Fixed by removing the kind kwarg.

BUG FIXED by tester (was crashing NewAppointmentDialog): /app/frontend/src/pages/provider/AppointmentsEHR.jsx had <SelectItem value=''>— Custom —</SelectItem> which Radix Select forbids; throwing 'Uncaught runtime errors' modal that hid the dialog. Replaced empty value with '__custom__' and updated default form.treatment_id accordingly.
Visit-mode literal mismatch (above) is a follow-up — frontend submits 'in_clinic' but backend Literal accepts 'in_person'/'telehealth'. POST /api/appointments will 422 if user picks 'In clinic'.
server.py is now 2569 lines — refactor still deferred (mentioned in iteration_2 already).

WS endpoint accepts JWT in URL query string. Tokens are short-lived but they still hit access logs and proxy logs (visible in /var/log/supervisor/backend.out.log). Consider a one-shot signed handshake token or moving auth to first WS message after accept().
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/pages/provider/AppointmentsEHR.jsx (replaced empty SelectItem value to fix Radix crash)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested EHR flows (Appointments, Wizard, Transactions EOD)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_p6_* clients via tests + 3 telehealth appointments (1 confirmed used by WS+chat+recording tests, 2 RBAC-only). Created 1 TEST UI Wiz P6 v2 patient via UI wizard.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 6 backend is fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_phase6.py (17 tests, includes async WebSocket tests using `websockets` lib + pytest-asyncio mode=auto). The WS auth fix was a 1-line tester fix to remove the kind='access' kwarg; the function signature in auth_utils.decode_token only takes (token: str). The visit_mode mismatch between frontend SelectItem ('in_clinic') and backend AppointmentIn.visit_mode literal ('in_person') was NOT fixed — will cause 422 if a user actually submits 'In clinic' from the dialog. Self-hosted WebRTC live media itself was not exercised (requires 2 browsers + camera) but signaling/chat/recording paths are all green.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE 1: ws_visit handler in /app/backend/server.py invoked `decode_token(token, kind='access')`, but /app/backend/auth_utils.py decode_token signature is `(token: str)`. The TypeError raised inside try/except Exception caused websocket.close(code=4401) before accept(), surfacing as HTTP 403 to clients. Reproduction: any websockets.connect(wss://.../api/ws/visit/{any}?token={any valid jwt}) -> HTTP 403. Mitigation applied: removed `kind='access'` arg. ROOT CAUSE 2: AppointmentsEHR NewAppointmentDialog declared <SelectItem value=''> for the Treatment-template Custom option. Radix UI Select hard-throws when value === '' because the empty string is reserved to clear-and-show-placeholder. The 'Uncaught runtime errors' overlay then blocked all subsequent UI tests. Reproduction: /portal/provider/appointments -> click any empty hour cell. Mitigation applied: value='__custom__' + matching default in form.treatment_id.
